disease.name: Pulmonary Hypertension
disease.note: Pulmonary hypertension is defined by mean pulmonary artery pressure >20 mmHg at rest on right heart catheterisation. WHO classification (Groups 1–5) guides aetiology and treatment. PAH-specific therapy includes prostacyclin analogues, endothelin receptor antagonists, and PDE-5 inhibitors.

immediateDangerSigns.description: Signs of low cardiac output, raised JVP, ascites, peripheral oedema
Unable to perform any physical activity without symptoms

historyFeatures.note: Exertional syncope suggests fixed cardiac output
Right ventricular angina due to RV ischaemia
Ascites due to right heart failure

examFeatures.note: Holosystolic at left lower sternal border

riskFactors.note: BMPR2 mutation accounts for 70% of heritable PAH
Aminorex, fenfluramine, dexfenfluramine, methamphetamine, cocaine, dasatinib
Heart failure with preserved or reduced EF, valvular disease

subtypes.name: Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (Group 1 PAH)
subtypes.management: PAH-specific therapy: calcium channel blockers (if acute vasoreactivity positive); ERA (bosentan/ambrisentan/macitentan); PDE-5 inhibitors (sildenafil/tadalafil); prostacyclin analogues (epoprostenol IV, treprostinil SC/IV, iloprost inhaled); combination therapy; lung transplant

subtypes.name: PH due to Left Heart Disease (Group 2)
subtypes.management: Treat underlying left heart disease; diuretics; PAH therapies NOT indicated (except in CpcPH under trial)

subtypes.name: PH due to Lung Disease/Hypoxia (Group 3)
subtypes.management: Optimise lung disease; LTOT; treat sleep-disordered breathing; NO routine PAH therapy (may worsen V/Q mismatch)

subtypes.name: Chronic Thromboembolic PH (Group 4 CTEPH)
subtypes.management: Anticoagulation lifelong; pulmonary endarterectomy (PEA) potentially curative; inhaled riociguat (sGC stimulator) if inoperable; balloon pulmonary angioplasty (BPA)

bedside.name: Six-minute walk test (6MWT)
bedside.indication: assess functional capacity and prognosis; distance <165 m predicts mortality
bedside.note: WHO class and 6MWT distance guide therapy escalation

laboratory.name: BNP or NT-proBNP
laboratory.indication: prognostic marker; correlates with RV function

laboratory.name: CTD serology (ANA, RF, anti-CCP, anti-Scl70, anti-RNP)

imaging.name: Echocardiogram (transthoracic) with Doppler
imaging.indication: estimate PAP (TR jet), RA size, RV size/function, pericardial effusion, left heart function
imaging.note: PASP >40 mmHg suggests PH; TAPSE, FAC, S' assess RV function

imaging.name: Chest X-ray
imaging.indication: prominent PA, RA enlargement, oligaemic lung fields

imaging.name: V/Q scan
imaging.indication: all PH patients should have V/Q to exclude CTEPH

imaging.name: HRCT chest
imaging.indication: if lung disease suspected as cause

advanced.name: Right heart catheterisation (RHC)
advanced.indication: definitive diagnosis; mPAP >20 mmHg confirms PH
advanced.note: Measure mPAP, PCWP (≤15 excludes group 2), PVR, cardiac output, RA pressure, acute vasoreactivity testing (nitric oxide)

advanced.name: Pulmonary function tests (spirometry, lung volumes, DLCO)
advanced.indication: assess lung function contribution; reduced DLCO out of proportion to restriction suggests PAH

advanced.name: Cardiac MRI
advanced.indication: RV volumetric assessment, stroke volume, late gadolinium enhancement

management.protocolReference: ERS/ESC Guidelines on Pulmonary Hypertension (2022/2024); WHO PH Classification

pathways.severity: mild (WHO class I–II, low REVEAL risk)
pathways.criteria: 6MWD >440 m, RA pressure <8, CI >2.5, low BNP
treatment: If positive acute vasoreactivity (fall in mPAP ≥10 mmHg to ≤40 mmHg with preserved CO): high-dose CCB (nifedipine or diltiazem)
If negative or no sustained response: initiate PAH-specific oral monotherapy (ERA or PDE-5i)
General: diuretics if fluid overload, warfarin in IPAH (controversial), oxygen if hypoxaemic
Supervised exercise training and pulmonary rehabilitation
Avoid pregnancy, high altitude, heavy isometric exertion

pathways.criteria: 6MWD 165–440 m, RA pressure 8–14, CI 2.0–2.5, BNP elevated
treatment: Initial oral combination therapy: ERA + PDE-5 inhibitor (e.g., ambrisentan + tadalafil, bosentan + sildenafil)
If inadequate response: triple therapy with prostacyclin pathway agent (selexipag oral or IV/SC prostacyclin)
Diuretics aggressively for right heart failure
Consider atrial septostomy if refractory with recurrent syncope (bridge to transplant)
pathways.followUp: 3-monthly reassessment; escalate therapy if intermediate risk persists

pathways.criteria: 6MWD <165 m, RA pressure >14, CI <2.0, BNP very high, right heart failure
treatment: Parenteral prostacyclin analogue: IV epoprostenol (continuous infusion via tunneled catheter) or SC/IV treprostinil
Combination oral ERA + PDE-5i plus parenteral prostacyclin
Intensified diuretic therapy, inotropes if low cardiac output
Lung transplant evaluation (bilateral lung or heart-lung for advanced PAH)
Palliative care integration if not transplant candidate
Potassium supplementation and careful fluid management
pathways.escalation: Urgent lung transplant referral. Atrial septostomy as bridge. ECMO as bridge to transplant in refractory cases.

specialConsiderations.Group_2_PH: Treat left heart disease; PAH therapies not recommended (may cause pulmonary oedema)
specialConsiderations.Group_3_PH: Treat underlying lung disease; LTOT; no routine PAH therapy
specialConsiderations.Group_4_CTEPH: Lifelong anticoagulation; pulmonary endarterectomy is potentially curative; riociguat if inoperable
specialConsiderations.Group_5_PH: Treat underlying condition (sarcoidosis, metabolic, haematologic, systemic)

disease.emergencyPriority: if right heart failure decompensation, refractory syncope, or WHO class IV with low cardiac output
diagnosticClues: exertional dyspnoea out of proportion to lung function
loud P2, RV heave, raised JVP
reduced DLCO with preserved lung volumes
mPAP >20 mmHg on RHC with PCWP ≤15
enlarged PA on CXR with RV hypertrophy on echo
exclusionClues: normal echo with estimated PAP <35 mmHg and no RV abnormality
left heart disease with elevated PCWP >15 suggests group 2 PH
PFTs showing obstruction or restriction suggests group 3 cause
